key_insight: PathAgentBench shows that vision-language models can reason well from curated pathology evidence, but they still struggle to find that evidence directly in whole-slide images.
clinical_relevance: For precision oncology workflows, the main gap is not just interpretation of pathology findings, but reliably locating diagnostically relevant regions across magnifications.
method: Built a benchmark around whole-slide pathology slide exploration rather than pre-cropped patches or pre-extracted features
Evaluated four tasks: image-to-text matching, text-to-image retrieval, diagnostic-region localization, and multi-scale reasoning
Used 1,822 TCGA WSIs plus 17,135 annotated diagnostic paths, with an additional private breast cancer cohort for autonomous exploration testing
evidence_signal: The benchmark evaluated 20 models; open-weight models exceeded 93% accuracy in multi-scale reasoning and 50%+ in cross-modal matching, but best localization mIoU stayed below 0.09 and autonomous hit rates fell to 0.020 at high magnification.
watch_item: Strong reasoning scores may overstate real-world utility because evidence acquisition on gigapixel WSIs remained poor, especially for high-magnification target finding.
paper_title: PathAgentBench: Benchmarking Evidence-Seeking Vision-Language Models on Whole-Slide Pathology Image
